metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=ready — UNBLOCKED by subagent linking (antigravity_subagent.go). agy dispatches a fire-and-forget background subagent (run_subagent 'runs in the background, reports back when done'); the parent finishes its own reply while a detached child keeps working. The child is its own brain/<child>/ conversation (transcript_new), linked via AntigravityParentConvID → parent_linked. Per the unified rule the SESSION stays working (reevaluateParent / hasActiveChildren) even though the parent's own reply reached end_turn; SubagentCount includes the live child; working→ready only after the last child drains. Same daemon mechanism as 3.1, only the timing differs (parent end_turn lands BEFORE child drain). Capable model required.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** Background subagents are exactly what `run_subagent` is: the v1.0.10 schema says 'each subagent runs in the BACKGROUND with its own prompt and reports back when done,' and the binary carries `GetSubagents`/`HasSubagents`/`KillSubagent` plus the IDE 'Background Tasks' pane. The parent dispatches one (or several) fire-and-forget children via `invoke_subagent` and FINISHES ITS OWN REPLY (acknowledge + report back) without blocking on results. Requires a capable model (Gemini 3.1 Pro High / 3.5 Flash); GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the invoke_subagent schema.

**daemon=full (UNBLOCKED).** Previously frozen `incapable`. With subagent linking the prerequisites now exist and the SAME daemon mechanism as foreground-subagent applies — the only difference is timing. The background child runs as its own top-level conversation `brain/<child>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl` → its own session via `FilesUnderRoot`/`sessionIDFromPath` (transcript_new). `AntigravityParentConvID` (antigravity_subagent.go) finds the parent via its `INVOKE_SUBAGENT` step referencing the child conv-id → `parent_linked` (wired in helpers.go:102). The distinguishing observable: the parent's OWN reply reaches end_turn (a terminal `PLANNER_RESPONSE` with no tool_calls) BEFORE the child drains, yet the SESSION stays `working` because `reevaluateParent` holds it on `hasActiveChildren`; `ComputeSubagentSummary` counts the live child. The session transitions `working → ready` ONLY after the last background child drains. LIVE-VERIFIED (the linking mechanism): a spawned subagent linked child.parent_session_id=parent, parent summary total:1 working:1, parent held working.

**driver=ready.** A single `send` (dispatch background child(ren) + report immediately) + `wait_turn` (parent's own reply ends) + a settle `sleep` that holds the child fresh under the SubagentQuietWindow. All implemented in `driver-interactive.sh`.
caveats: daemon=full is derived from the linking code + LIVE-VERIFIED linking note; the held-working-past-parent-end_turn timing for antigravity's BACKGROUND shape is PROVISIONAL until record confirms the parent does NOT go ready while the child is alive (confidence 0.7).
The background child has no own agy process — it stays pid=0 (transcript-first). Assert ParentSessionID linkage + held-working + subagent summary, NOT a child PID.

FAITHFULNESS: if the recording shows the parent blocked until the child finished (parent end_turn AFTER child drain), the dispatch ran foreground — re-craft the send to force background + report-immediately, do NOT accept it as background.
observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage.

sources.note: AntigravityParentConvID links the background child to the parent via the parent's INVOKE_SUBAGENT step — the link that enables held-working
deriveParentSession calls AntigravityParentConvID — wiring point; the standard reevaluateParent/hasActiveChildren machinery then holds the parent working
Parent's terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE no-tool would settle it ready — but hasActiveChildren overrides; the recording must confirm the override
run_subagent 'runs in the background ... reports back when done'; Background Tasks pane

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
A CAPABLE model selected (Gemini 3.1 Pro High or Gemini 3.5 Flash) — GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the invoke_subagent schema
irrlichd in --record mode with grant-all permission mode (transcripts permission)
setup: Driver launches agy under tmux in a fresh workspace cwd; the parent conv-id session binds pid>0 (history.jsonl cwd).

The single send dispatches a background subagent doing ~15-20s of work and instructs the parent to acknowledge immediately and report back WITHOUT waiting, so the parent's end_turn lands mid-child; the settle sleep holds the child fresh under the SubagentQuietWindow so the daemon doesn't fast-forward a still-writing child out of the held-working window.

script.text: Dispatch a background subagent to count the files in this directory and summarize them — do NOT wait for it. As soon as you've dispatched it, reply with exactly: dispatched

verify: the parent conv-id session: ready → working at dispatch
a background child session appears (transcript_new) and parent_linked binds child → parent
the parent's own reply reaches end_turn but the SESSION stays working (held by hasActiveChildren) — NO ready transition while the child is alive
SubagentCount(parent) includes the live background child
working → ready only after the last background child drains; final state ready
